mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE is below 4.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate synchronized real-economy recovery, which validates earnings revisions and triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized rate confirms robust aggregate demand expansion, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds the threshold, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of trading days, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation with SPX 5-day returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 258.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 45% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and rising retail sales volumes confirm broad-based income expansion and consumption strength, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive alpha in more than 50% of rolling 2-year windows, or the cross-sectional rank IC drops below 0.02 when tested against the S&P 500.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging above 1.6 million annualized units signal strong builder confidence and household formation, which historically precedes broad equity market rallies and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where HOUST crosses above 1600.0, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression over 10 years of data.
